results.rendered_html: sārva—dhātuka mfn. (fr. sarva-dhātu) applicable to the whole of a radical term or to the complete form of the verbal base (after the conjugational characteristics or Vikaraṇas are affixed in the four conj. or special tenses; in Veda often confounded with the Ārdhadhātukas q.v.)

results.rendered_html: sārva—dhātuka n. N. of the verbal terminations of the four conj. tenses (Pr., Impf. Pot., and Impv.), and of all the root affixes (such as śānac and śatri) which have an indicatory ṣ,  Pāṇ. i, 2, 4 ; iii, 4, 113  &c.

results.rendered_html:
sārvadhātuka (von sarva + dhātu) adj. der ganzen Wurzel d. i. dem Verbalthema, wie es in den Special-Formen erscheint, zukommend, daran gefügt werdend  P. 3,4,113 . 1,2,4 . 6,1,186 . 192 . 4,87 . 110 . 7,2,76 . 3,84 . 87 . 95 . 4,21 . Vārtt. zu 3,1,78 .
— Vgl. ārdhadhātuka.

results.rendered_html:
sārvadhātuka a. (kī f.) Applicable to the whole of a radical term, or to the complete form of the verbal base after the conjugational characteristics have been affixed, i. e. to the four conjugational or special tenses.
— kaṃ N. of the verbal terminations of the four conjugational tenses (strictly, the personal terminations of all tenses and moods except the Perfect and Benedictive and the affixes distinguished by a mute ś).
